other_observers: none; I am the original finder.
specific_location: South side of Cottonwood Marsh at Walden Ponds
description: This bird is a modest-sized flycatcher (7”) with distinctive black to dark gray plumage on its head, back, throat, upper breast, wings, tail. The belly and undertail coverts are white with the white coming to a point on the breast. The back of the head has a slight peak and the tail is long.Behaviors: The bird was perched on a low branch at the edge of Cottonwood Marsh. While perched it flicked its tail. It then moved to another low, exposed perch.Call: nonePlumage: basic
